Per-batter detail

1.Sal StewartRHBCIN vs Joey Cantillo (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.169 / xBA 0.237 / xSLG 0.414 (-146 pts)K 25.1%BB 10.6%HR 7.4%Hit 10.6%Strong Pitcher

Sal Stewart RHB vs Joey Cantillo LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 741+240+0, pitcher rows 753+1681+759

Verdict

Cantillo's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Sal Stewart should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.169 xwOBA (-146 pts vs league, Pitcher). Count-state blend: -148 pts (bb_type-adj 0.314 -> final 0.169); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Sal Stewart is on a 14-day slump (-45 pts vs season). Joey Cantillo is in a 14-day hot stretch (-46 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Pitch-mix projection

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
4-Seam Fastball43.80.3340.349GB 36% / LD 20% / FB 35% / PU 9%0.332-20.3660.2520.51912.1
Changeup32.30.3130.269GB 48% / LD 21% / FB 26% / PU 5%0.304-90.2580.2040.30564.6
Curveball19.20.3180.279GB 68% / LD 23% / FB 9% / PU 0%0.304-140.2680.2400.3220.0
Slider4.60.3180.331GB 27% / LD 42% / FB 30% / PU 0%0.394+760.4100.3280.55721.1

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

Bat-tracking interaction

bat speed 70.4 mph
swing length 7.7 ft
attack angle 13.1°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-5.2+7.412+2.2on plane
Changeup-7.3+17.75+10.4swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Curveball-10.3+13.1*2+2.8on plane
Slider-7.8+13.1*5+5.3swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

2.Drew RomoLHBCWS vs Jameson Taillon (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.205 / xBA 0.242 / xSLG 0.450 (-110 pts)K 21.3%BB 8.7%HR 4.3%Hit 11.0%Strong Pitcher

Drew Romo LHB vs Jameson Taillon RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 164+12+189, pitcher rows 744+2114+2483

Verdict

Taillon's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Drew Romo should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.205 xwOBA (-110 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +8 pts (raw 0.321 -> adj 0.328); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -124 pts (bb_type-adj 0.328 -> final 0.205); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Drew Romo is on a 14-day slump (-77 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

3.Joe MackLHBMIA vs Nick Martinez (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.212 / xBA 0.246 / xSLG 0.395 (-103 pts)K 21.6%BB 5.0%HR 0.0%Hit 16.2%Strong Pitcher

Joe Mack LHB vs Nick Martinez RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 133+0+0, pitcher rows 669+2603+2126

Verdict

Martinez's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Joe Mack should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.212 xwOBA (-103 pts vs league, Pitcher). Count-state blend: -87 pts (bb_type-adj 0.305 -> final 0.212); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

4.Kevin McGonigleLHBDET vs Mason Fluharty (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.239 / xBA 0.189 / xSLG 0.320 (-76 pts)K 30.0%BB 12.2%HR 1.8%Hit 12.9%Edge Pitcher

Kevin McGonigle LHB vs Mason Fluharty LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 759+0+0, pitcher rows 284+1017+0

Verdict

Fluharty's cutter is the projected matchup advantage. Projection: 0.239 xwOBA (-76 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -22 pts (raw 0.286 -> adj 0.264); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -28 pts (bb_type-adj 0.264 -> final 0.239); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Kevin McGonigle is on a 14-day slump (-68 pts vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
Sweeper53.60.3200.272GB 39% / LD 11% / FB 45% / PU 5%0.311-90.2680.1890.35524.2
Cutter46.40.3100.301GB 37% / LD 23% / FB 17% / PU 23%0.273-370.2590.1890.27917.8

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

5.Jose SiriRHBLAA vs Justin Wrobleski (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.246 / xBA 0.248 / xSLG 0.415 (-69 pts)K 24.7%BB 6.1%HR 4.6%Hit 15.6%Edge Pitcher

Jose Siri RHB vs Justin Wrobleski LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 0+163+1790, pitcher rows 618+1175+593

Verdict

Wrobleski's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Jose Siri should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.246 xwOBA (-69 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +7 pts (raw 0.310 -> adj 0.317); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -70 pts (bb_type-adj 0.317 -> final 0.246); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

swing length 7.0 ft

PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. 7.Henry BolteRHBATH vs Trevor McDonald (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.249 / xBA 0.217 / xSLG 0.257 (-66 pts)K 24.1%BB 1.9%HR 0.0%Hit 26.8%Edge Pitcher

Henry Bolte RHB vs Trevor McDonald RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 39+0+0, pitcher rows 182+213+39

Verdict

McDonald's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Henry Bolte should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.249 xwOBA (-66 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -56 pts (raw 0.296 -> adj 0.240); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Zach Cole LHB vs Jacob deGrom RHP

Verdict

deGrom's slider is the projected matchup advantage. Projection: 0.261 xwOBA (-54 pts vs league, Pitcher). Count-state blend: -31 pts (bb_type-adj 0.295 -> final 0.261); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

9.Luis TorrensRHBNYM vs Carlos Rodón (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.264 / xBA 0.193 / xSLG 0.331 (-51 pts)K 23.6%BB 8.5%HR 0.1%Hit 18.5%Edge Pitcher

Luis Torrens RHB vs Carlos Rodón LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 223+1070+513, pitcher rows 78+3375+3415

Verdict

Rodón's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Luis Torrens should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.264 xwOBA (-51 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -47 pts (raw 0.311 -> adj 0.264); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

10.Munetaka MurakamiLHBCWS vs Jameson Taillon (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.264 / xBA 0.272 / xSLG 0.447 (-51 pts)K 24.4%BB 11.1%HR 5.2%Hit 14.5%Edge Pitcher

Munetaka Murakami LHB vs Jameson Taillon RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 811+0+0, pitcher rows 744+2114+2483

Verdict

Taillon's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Munetaka Murakami should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.264 xwOBA (-51 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +7 pts (raw 0.335 -> adj 0.342); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -79 pts (bb_type-adj 0.342 -> final 0.264); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Munetaka Murakami is on a 14-day slump (-54 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

11.Owen CaissieLHBMIA vs Nick Martinez (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.265 / xBA 0.240 / xSLG 0.371 (-50 pts)K 23.9%BB 5.1%HR 2.0%Hit 21.6%Slight Pitcher

Owen Caissie LHB vs Nick Martinez RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 519+103+0, pitcher rows 669+2603+2126

Verdict

Martinez's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Owen Caissie should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.265 xwOBA (-50 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -14 pts (raw 0.307 -> adj 0.294); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -21 pts (bb_type-adj 0.294 -> final 0.265); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

12.Shea LangeliersRHBATH vs Trevor McDonald (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.270 / xBA 0.224 / xSLG 0.283 (-45 pts)K 24.5%BB 2.0%HR 4.6%Hit 21.1%Slight Pitcher

Shea Langeliers RHB vs Trevor McDonald RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 671+1930+2003, pitcher rows 182+213+39

Verdict

McDonald's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Shea Langeliers should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.270 xwOBA (-45 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -71 pts (raw 0.318 -> adj 0.247); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +13 pts (bb_type-adj 0.247 -> final 0.270); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Shea Langeliers is on a 14-day heater (+108 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
Sinker50.50.3590.305GB 73% / LD 19% / FB 0% / PU 8%0.242-1170.2320.2310.2283.8
Slider45.50.3120.288GB 59% / LD 13% / FB 24% / PU 4%0.291-210.2640.2160.34750.9
Cutter3.00.3310.283GB 68% / LD 16% / FB 11% / PU 6%0.272-590.2400.2340.25228.6
Changeup1.00.3140.283GB 68% / LD 16% / FB 11% / PU 6%0.260-530.2280.2090.25630.9

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

13.Zach McKinstryLHBDET vs Mason Fluharty (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.271 / xBA 0.189 / xSLG 0.362 (-44 pts)K 31.1%BB 10.0%HR 1.7%Hit 14.4%Slight Pitcher

Zach McKinstry LHB vs Mason Fluharty LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 296+2180+1278, pitcher rows 284+1017+0

Verdict

Fluharty's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Zach McKinstry should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.271 xwOBA (-44 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -9 pts (raw 0.284 -> adj 0.275); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -7 pts (bb_type-adj 0.275 -> final 0.271); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

14.Jared TrioloRHBPIT vs Cristopher Sánchez (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.272 / xBA 0.240 / xSLG 0.312 (-43 pts)K 22.8%BB 6.0%HR 0.0%Hit 23.7%Slight Pitcher

Jared Triolo RHB vs Cristopher Sánchez LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 155+1459+1765, pitcher rows 762+3086+2886

Verdict

Sánchez's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Jared Triolo should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.272 xwOBA (-43 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -30 pts (raw 0.300 -> adj 0.270); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +6 pts (bb_type-adj 0.270 -> final 0.272); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Cristopher Sánchez is in a 14-day hot stretch (-98 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Sinker44.30.3260.367GB 52% / LD 29% / FB 18% / PU 1%0.309-170.3610.3150.4393.4

Cutter0.10.3090.295GB 56% / LD 25% / FB 16% / PU 3%0.274-350.2540.2330.28311.5

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

15.Wenceel PérezLHBDET vs Mason Fluharty (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.273 / xBA 0.192 / xSLG 0.321 (-42 pts)K 29.9%BB 9.2%HR 6.6%Hit 12.2%Slight Pitcher

Wenceel Pérez LHB vs Mason Fluharty LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 337+1620+1829, pitcher rows 284+1017+0

Verdict

Fluharty's cutter is the projected matchup advantage, while Wenceel Pérez should look to drive the sweeper. Projection: 0.273 xwOBA (-42 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -21 pts (raw 0.285 -> adj 0.263); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +7 pts (bb_type-adj 0.263 -> final 0.273); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

16.Bryan ReynoldsLHBPIT vs Cristopher Sánchez (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.275 / xBA 0.218 / xSLG 0.317 (-40 pts)K 27.7%BB 5.0%HR 1.1%Hit 24.0%Slight Pitcher

Bryan Reynolds LHB vs Cristopher Sánchez LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 756+2636+2616, pitcher rows 762+3086+2886

Verdict

Sánchez's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Bryan Reynolds should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.275 xwOBA (-40 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -17 pts (raw 0.283 -> adj 0.266); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +12 pts (bb_type-adj 0.266 -> final 0.275); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Cristopher Sánchez is in a 14-day hot stretch (-98 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

17.Zack GelofRHBATH vs Trevor McDonald (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.276 / xBA 0.224 / xSLG 0.293 (-39 pts)K 30.5%BB 1.8%HR 3.1%Hit 24.1%Slight Pitcher

Zack Gelof RHB vs Trevor McDonald RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 308+416+2056, pitcher rows 182+213+39

Verdict

McDonald's slider is the projected matchup advantage. Projection: 0.276 xwOBA (-39 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -24 pts (raw 0.279 -> adj 0.256); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +9 pts (bb_type-adj 0.256 -> final 0.276); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Zack Gelof is on a 14-day heater (+54 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Sinker50.40.2970.305GB 73% / LD 19% / FB 0% / PU 8%0.288-90.2780.2470.3089.2

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Verdict

Sánchez's changeup is the projected matchup advantage. Projection: 0.278 xwOBA (-37 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -24 pts (raw 0.282 -> adj 0.258); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +24 pts (bb_type-adj 0.258 -> final 0.278); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Ryan O'Hearn is on a 14-day slump (-82 pts vs season). Cristopher Sánchez is in a 14-day hot stretch (-98 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

19.Christian VázquezRHBHOU vs Jacob deGrom (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.279 / xBA 0.233 / xSLG 0.387 (-36 pts)K 22.9%BB 3.3%HR 3.0%Hit 23.3%Slight Pitcher

Christian Vázquez RHB vs Jacob deGrom RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 345+834+1141, pitcher rows 712+2617+172

Verdict

deGrom's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Christian Vázquez should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.279 xwOBA (-36 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +14 pts (raw 0.277 -> adj 0.291); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -8 pts (bb_type-adj 0.291 -> final 0.279); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Christian Vázquez is on a 14-day slump (-51 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

21.Brent RookerRHBATH vs Trevor McDonald (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.280 / xBA 0.216 / xSLG 0.290 (-35 pts)K 27.3%BB 2.3%HR 3.5%Hit 23.4%Slight Pitcher

Brent Rooker RHB vs Trevor McDonald RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 407+2590+2391, pitcher rows 182+213+39

Verdict

McDonald's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Brent Rooker should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.280 xwOBA (-35 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -71 pts (raw 0.320 -> adj 0.249); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +20 pts (bb_type-adj 0.249 -> final 0.280); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

attack angle 4.0°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-4.3-0.814-5.1swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk
Cutter-6.0+4.0*2-1.9on plane
Sinker-5.2+4.0*2-1.2on plane
Curveball-8.4+12.26+3.8swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Changeup-6.7+9.08+2.2on plane
Slider-6.7+4.0*4-2.6on plane

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. 23.Brandon LoweLHBPIT vs Cristopher Sánchez (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.280 / xBA 0.224 / xSLG 0.315 (-35 pts)K 29.4%BB 3.3%HR 2.6%Hit 22.1%Slight Pitcher

Brandon Lowe LHB vs Cristopher Sánchez LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 710+2148+1632, pitcher rows 762+3086+2886

Verdict

Sánchez's slider is the projected matchup advantage. Projection: 0.280 xwOBA (-35 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -7 pts (raw 0.274 -> adj 0.267); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +17 pts (bb_type-adj 0.267 -> final 0.280); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Cristopher Sánchez is in a 14-day hot stretch (-98 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Sinker59.40.3270.278GB 73% / LD 16% / FB 8% / PU 2%0.327-00.2900.2410.34211.5

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Verdict

Buehler's sinker is the projected matchup advantage, while Jhonny Pereda should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.282 xwOBA (-33 pts vs league, Pitcher). Count-state blend: -23 pts (bb_type-adj 0.319 -> final 0.282); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. 25.Vaughn GrissomRHBLAA vs Justin Wrobleski (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.282 / xBA 0.250 / xSLG 0.401 (-33 pts)K 19.4%BB 7.8%HR 0.0%Hit 21.1%Slight Pitcher

Vaughn Grissom RHB vs Justin Wrobleski LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 350+0+444, pitcher rows 618+1175+593

Verdict

Wrobleski's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Vaughn Grissom should look to drive the curveball. Projection: 0.282 xwOBA (-33 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +6 pts (raw 0.311 -> adj 0.317); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -34 pts (bb_type-adj 0.317 -> final 0.282); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

26.Liam HicksLHBMIA vs Nick Martinez (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.283 / xBA 0.238 / xSLG 0.384 (-32 pts)K 12.9%BB 6.1%HR 2.2%Hit 23.0%Slight Pitcher

Liam Hicks LHB vs Nick Martinez RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 552+1537+0, pitcher rows 669+2603+2126

Verdict

Martinez's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Liam Hicks should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.283 xwOBA (-32 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -16 pts (raw 0.312 -> adj 0.297); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -6 pts (bb_type-adj 0.297 -> final 0.283); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

27.Xavier EdwardsLHBMIA vs Nick Martinez (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.284 / xBA 0.239 / xSLG 0.381 (-31 pts)K 12.7%BB 6.2%HR 0.7%Hit 24.1%Slight Pitcher

Xavier Edwards LHB vs Nick Martinez RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 723+2260+1192, pitcher rows 669+2603+2126

Verdict

Martinez's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Xavier Edwards should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.284 xwOBA (-31 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -12 pts (raw 0.308 -> adj 0.296); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

28.Jake RogersRHBDET vs Mason Fluharty (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.285 / xBA 0.228 / xSLG 0.363 (-30 pts)K 21.5%BB 9.2%HR 4.2%Hit 19.1%Slight Pitcher

Jake Rogers RHB vs Mason Fluharty LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 168+574+1362, pitcher rows 284+1017+0

Verdict

Fluharty's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Jake Rogers should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.285 xwOBA (-30 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -21 pts (raw 0.311 -> adj 0.290); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -8 pts (bb_type-adj 0.290 -> final 0.285); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

29.Gary SánchezRHBMIL vs Connor Prielipp (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.286 / xBA 0.238 / xSLG 0.407 (-29 pts)K 29.4%BB 10.4%HR 2.8%Hit 16.8%Slight Pitcher

Gary Sánchez RHB vs Connor Prielipp LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 450+415+1186, pitcher rows 351+0+0

Verdict

Prielipp's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Gary Sánchez should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.286 xwOBA (-29 pts vs league, Pitcher). Count-state blend: -29 pts (bb_type-adj 0.318 -> final 0.286); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Gary Sánchez is on a 14-day slump (-146 pts vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

30.Nick CastellanosRHBSD vs Logan Gilbert (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.287 / xBA 0.234 / xSLG 0.384 (-28 pts)K 25.4%BB 2.8%HR 3.5%Hit 23.4%Slight Pitcher

Nick Castellanos RHB vs Logan Gilbert RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 349+2229+2403, pitcher rows 733+2412+3043

Verdict

Gilbert's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Nick Castellanos should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.287 xwOBA (-28 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -16 pts (raw 0.305 -> adj 0.289); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +12 pts (bb_type-adj 0.289 -> final 0.287); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Nick Castellanos is on a 14-day heater (+49 pts vs season). Logan Gilbert is in a 14-day rough patch (+71 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

31.Luis RengifoLHBMIL vs Connor Prielipp (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.288 / xBA 0.227 / xSLG 0.414 (-27 pts)K 11.1%BB 8.3%HR 3.2%Hit 19.9%Slight Pitcher

Luis Rengifo LHB vs Connor Prielipp LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 489+2011+1086, pitcher rows 351+0+0

Verdict

Prielipp's sinker is the projected matchup advantage, while Luis Rengifo should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.288 xwOBA (-27 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -17 pts (raw 0.321 -> adj 0.304); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -13 pts (bb_type-adj 0.304 -> final 0.288); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Luis Rengifo is on a 14-day heater (+46 pts vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Pitch-mix projection

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
Slider48.20.3170.321GB 42% / LD 24% / FB 27% / PU 7%0.303-140.3090.2300.42531.3
4-Seam Fastball29.90.3090.324GB 42% / LD 24% / FB 27% / PU 7%0.289-210.2980.2210.4030.0
Sinker16.00.3240.311GB 42% / LD 24% / FB 27% / PU 7%0.306-180.3020.2290.3990.0
Curveball5.80.3110.324GB 42% / LD 24% / FB 27% / PU 7%0.298-130.3060.2250.42117.8

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Tolle's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Sandy León should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.289 xwOBA (-26 pts vs league, Pitcher). Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

33.Zach DezenzoRHBHOU vs Jacob deGrom (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.289 / xBA 0.228 / xSLG 0.386 (-26 pts)K 29.6%BB 4.4%HR 1.6%Hit 23.4%Slight Pitcher

Zach Dezenzo RHB vs Jacob deGrom RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 88+480+245, pitcher rows 712+2617+172

Verdict

deGrom's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Zach Dezenzo should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.289 xwOBA (-26 pts vs league, Pitcher). Count-state blend: +5 pts (bb_type-adj 0.288 -> final 0.289); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. 34.Konnor GriffinRHBPIT vs Cristopher Sánchez (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.290 / xBA 0.258 / xSLG 0.357 (-25 pts)K 24.8%BB 6.0%HR 0.0%Hit 28.1%Even

Konnor Griffin RHB vs Cristopher Sánchez LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 579+0+0, pitcher rows 762+3086+2886

Verdict

Sánchez's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Konnor Griffin should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.290 xwOBA (-25 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -9 pts (raw 0.302 -> adj 0.294); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Konnor Griffin is on a 14-day heater (+60 pts vs season). Cristopher Sánchez is in a 14-day hot stretch (-98 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Verdict

Prielipp's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Jackson Chourio should look to drive the changeup. Projection: 0.290 xwOBA (-25 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -18 pts (raw 0.310 -> adj 0.291); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. 36.Hunter FeducciaLHBTB vs Sandy Alcantara (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.291 / xBA 0.268 / xSLG 0.454 (-24 pts)K 16.0%BB 15.0%HR 0.0%Hit 18.6%Even

Hunter Feduccia LHB vs Sandy Alcantara RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 234+477+57, pitcher rows 865+2893+0

Verdict

Alcantara's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Hunter Feduccia should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.291 xwOBA (-24 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +24 pts (raw 0.324 -> adj 0.348); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -49 pts (bb_type-adj 0.348 -> final 0.291); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Sandy Alcantara is in a 14-day rough patch (+49 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

37.Carson BengeLHBNYM vs Carlos Rodón (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.291 / xBA 0.230 / xSLG 0.378 (-24 pts)K 27.8%BB 6.4%HR 0.0%Hit 28.0%Even

Carson Benge LHB vs Carlos Rodón LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 559+0+0, pitcher rows 78+3375+3415

Verdict

Rodón's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Carson Benge should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.291 xwOBA (-24 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -7 pts (raw 0.303 -> adj 0.297); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Carson Benge is on a 14-day heater (+83 pts vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

39.Mauricio DubónRHBATL vs Payton Tolle (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.293 / xBA 0.228 / xSLG 0.392 (-22 pts)K 23.3%BB 6.5%HR 0.6%Hit 25.4%Even

Mauricio Dubón RHB vs Payton Tolle LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 622+1346+1463, pitcher rows 325+311+0

Verdict

Tolle's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Mauricio Dubón should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.293 xwOBA (-22 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +11 pts (raw 0.291 -> adj 0.302); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -7 pts (bb_type-adj 0.302 -> final 0.293); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

40.Nick GonzalesRHBPIT vs Cristopher Sánchez (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.293 / xBA 0.258 / xSLG 0.347 (-22 pts)K 23.0%BB 5.9%HR 1.3%Hit 23.4%Even

Nick Gonzales RHB vs Cristopher Sánchez LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 583+1410+1376, pitcher rows 762+3086+2886

Verdict

Sánchez's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Nick Gonzales should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.293 xwOBA (-22 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -19 pts (raw 0.309 -> adj 0.290); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +7 pts (bb_type-adj 0.290 -> final 0.293); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Cristopher Sánchez is in a 14-day hot stretch (-98 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
Sinker44.80.3190.367GB 52% / LD 29% / FB 18% / PU 1%0.320+10.3720.3250.4643.2
Changeup40.30.3240.222GB 65% / LD 20% / FB 11% / PU 4%0.284-400.1910.1850.19547.5
Slider14.80.3170.333GB 38% / LD 28% / FB 28% / PU 6%0.293-240.3110.2550.40928.5
Cutter0.10.3210.295GB 56% / LD 25% / FB 16% / PU 3%0.302-190.2820.2540.3347.8

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

bat speed 68.6 mph
swing length 7.5 ft

PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
Sinker-6.6+2.922-3.6swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk

Slider-8.4+13.125+4.7swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

41.Austin WellsLHBNYY vs Huascar Brazobán (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.294 / xBA 0.218 / xSLG 0.342 (-21 pts)K 18.3%BB 16.9%HR 2.8%Hit 15.2%Even

Austin Wells LHB vs Huascar Brazobán RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 516+1887+1853, pitcher rows 325+1017+903

Verdict

Brazobán's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Austin Wells should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.294 xwOBA (-21 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -22 pts (raw 0.304 -> adj 0.283); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +17 pts (bb_type-adj 0.283 -> final 0.294); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

42.Mike TroutRHBLAA vs Justin Wrobleski (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.294 / xBA 0.255 / xSLG 0.411 (-21 pts)K 24.1%BB 9.2%HR 3.4%Hit 20.0%Even

Mike Trout RHB vs Justin Wrobleski LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 834+2511+578, pitcher rows 618+1175+593

Verdict

Wrobleski's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Mike Trout should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.294 xwOBA (-21 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +9 pts (raw 0.314 -> adj 0.324); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -29 pts (bb_type-adj 0.324 -> final 0.294); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Mike Trout is on a 14-day slump (-115 pts vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

bat speed 72.8 mph
swing length 7.7 ft
attack angle 6.9°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-4.3+1.563-2.7on plane
Slider-7.4+8.814+1.4on plane
Curveball-8.6+6.9*4-1.8on plane
Cutter-5.3+9.519+4.2swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Sinker-5.0+0.311-4.7swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk
Changeup-6.9+17.025+10.1swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Sweeper-8.0+6.9*5-1.1on plane

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

43.Jose TrevinoRHBCIN vs Joey Cantillo (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.294 / xBA 0.249 / xSLG 0.420 (-21 pts)K 21.4%BB 8.8%HR 1.6%Hit 21.3%Even

Jose Trevino RHB vs Joey Cantillo LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 87+1063+913, pitcher rows 753+1681+759

Verdict

Cantillo's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Jose Trevino should look to drive the slider. Projection: 0.294 xwOBA (-21 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +27 pts (raw 0.296 -> adj 0.323); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -35 pts (bb_type-adj 0.323 -> final 0.294); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Joey Cantillo is in a 14-day hot stretch (-46 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

45.Austin MartinRHBMIN vs Logan Henderson (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.296 / xBA 0.238 / xSLG 0.392 (-19 pts)K 32.3%BB 5.7%HR 0.4%Hit 26.2%Even

Austin Martin RHB vs Logan Henderson RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 533+749+934, pitcher rows 185+410+0

Verdict

Henderson's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Austin Martin should look to drive the slider. Projection: 0.296 xwOBA (-19 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -5 pts (raw 0.316 -> adj 0.310); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -12 pts (bb_type-adj 0.310 -> final 0.296); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Austin Martin is on a 14-day slump (-46 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

46.Miguel VargasRHBCWS vs Jameson Taillon (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.297 / xBA 0.234 / xSLG 0.396 (-18 pts)K 15.2%BB 4.0%HR 3.2%Hit 23.7%Even

Miguel Vargas RHB vs Jameson Taillon RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 730+2326+1009, pitcher rows 744+2114+2483

Verdict

Taillon's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Miguel Vargas should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.297 xwOBA (-18 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -9 pts (raw 0.312 -> adj 0.302); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -7 pts (bb_type-adj 0.302 -> final 0.297); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Miguel Vargas is on a 14-day heater (+89 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. 47.Cody BellingerLHBNYY vs Huascar Brazobán (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.297 / xBA 0.225 / xSLG 0.338 (-18 pts)K 12.0%BB 16.5%HR 1.5%Hit 17.3%Even

Cody Bellinger LHB vs Huascar Brazobán RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 697+2697+2109, pitcher rows 325+1017+903

Verdict

Brazobán's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Cody Bellinger should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.297 xwOBA (-18 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -25 pts (raw 0.314 -> adj 0.289); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +14 pts (bb_type-adj 0.289 -> final 0.297); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

48.Oneil CruzLHBPIT vs Cristopher Sánchez (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.297 / xBA 0.215 / xSLG 0.317 (-18 pts)K 32.1%BB 4.3%HR 2.1%Hit 25.0%Even

Oneil Cruz LHB vs Cristopher Sánchez LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 764+2250+2404, pitcher rows 762+3086+2886

Verdict

Sánchez's sinker is the projected matchup advantage. Projection: 0.297 xwOBA (-18 pts vs league, Pitcher). Count-state blend: +34 pts (bb_type-adj 0.267 -> final 0.297); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Cristopher Sánchez is in a 14-day hot stretch (-98 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

50.Xander BogaertsRHBSD vs Logan Gilbert (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.297 / xBA 0.243 / xSLG 0.407 (-18 pts)K 23.2%BB 4.0%HR 3.4%Hit 24.3%Even

Xander Bogaerts RHB vs Logan Gilbert RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 643+2165+1860, pitcher rows 733+2412+3043

Verdict

Gilbert's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Xander Bogaerts should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.297 xwOBA (-18 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -13 pts (raw 0.321 -> adj 0.309); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Logan Gilbert is in a 14-day rough patch (+71 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Pitcherlast 30d490.3950.3470.56318.42.023.151.4

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

Coverage % = share of the pitcher's attack on this pitch landing in zones with batter data; remainder imputed at league baselines (xwOBA 0.315, xBA 0.245, xSLG 0.405) so two batters with different coverage maps stay comparable.

bat speed 69.2 mph
swing length 7.2 ft
attack angle 9.6°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-4.8+5.7138+0.9on plane
Slider-7.8+13.488+5.6swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).
